note: Honest coverage frontier. status: present = a runnable step drives real code and passes (must name a test); pending = specified behavior whose implementation (core/credential.Engine, Phase 2) does not exist yet; retrospective_gap = shipped without a driving test (must be zero for governed behavior, INV-22). Nothing is built yet, so every scenario is pending — this is the design gate (TG-107) for epic TG-107; scenarios bind to real code as tasks T-016-* land.

scenarios.name: A target resolves to exactly one credential bundle from config data
A credential bundle is constructible only with all required fields
An unresolved target is refused with no default identity
The fail-closed property holds under an empty or partially-synced store
Every credential value is a SecretRef resolved at runtime and no plaintext is stored
Identity is resolved after a non-deny policy verdict and before execute
The resolver matches on the same estate object-model as the policy engine
A multi-rule target resolves by most-specific-wins and an equal-specificity conflict refuses
A source syncs read-only into the native store on schedule and on demand
A repeated sync of unchanged data converges with no duplicate or orphan
A target in multiple sources resolves by source precedence and records shadowed sources
A target with no synced source resolves from the native-store fallback
A machine-plane source cannot populate approver identity
An approver-plane source cannot populate a host credential bundle
The AWX Ansible Semaphore source pulls inventory and credentials read-only with no subprocess
scenarios.note: Bound via TWO real connectors of the disjunctive family ('an AWX Ansible OR Semaphore platform'), T-016-8: (1) the AWX REST connector (modules/credsource/awx) proves the read-only inventory+credential pull with no subprocess against a fake AWX API (native net/http); (2) the UN-CONTROLLED Ansible connector (modules/credsource/ansible) proves the plain-files case — it parses a real inventory + group_vars/host_vars tree and NATIVELY decrypts an inline !vault $ANSIBLE_VAULT payload to its known plaintext in pure Go (no ansible-vault CLI, no subprocess), with the become secret carried as an ansible-vault: locator (INV-13). The Semaphore connector is covered by its own package tests.

scenarios.name: The vault bao SecretRef scheme reads KV v2 read-only and fails closed on unreachable or denied or expired
The oidc SecretRef scheme mints a machine-plane client-credentials token and fails closed on unreachable or denied or untrusted-TLS
The LDAP OIDC source pulls approver identities read-only and never writes a host bundle
Each source records last-synced and drift
Read-only fact-gathering is a Phase-1-safe sensor and playbook actuation routes through the mode chokepoint
Every resolution and sync is appended to the governance ledger with no secret value
The credential tables are append-only with no runtime UPDATE or DELETE
A source is admitted only when its connector adapter is registered at startup
The console renders per-source last-synced and drift
The console configures tests schedules and syncs each source on demand
The console renders the per-target credential map with source and precedence
The console coverage view answers whether TG can reach a target
The console object-groups editor is shared with the policy engine
A secret value is write-only in the console and never echoed
